name: lunacode
description: An agentic coding assistant powered by OpenRouter. Configure with just an API key and a model.

commands.title: Luna Code: Open in Editor Tab
Luna Code: Open in Secondary Side Bar
Luna Code: Set OpenRouter API Key
Luna Code: Clear OpenRouter API Key
Luna Code: Add Selection to Chat
Luna Code: Fix Problems in This File
Luna Code: Export Session as Markdown

lunacode.model.description: OpenRouter model id used for agentic coding sessions. Use the model picker (or browse the live list at https://openrouter.ai/models) for current ids.

lunacode.baseUrl.description: OpenRouter API base URL. Override only if you use a proxy.

enumDescriptions: Standard — approve each command/edit before it runs.
Auto — run edits and commands autonomously without prompting (always-deny commands are still blocked).
Plan — read-only research and planning; never edits files or runs mutating commands.
lunacode.defaultMode.description: Default agent mode for new sessions.

lunacode.maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens to generate per assistant turn. 0 (recommended) lets the provider use the model's full output limit, so write_file can emit large files without truncating. Set a positive number only to cap cost — but too low truncates tool calls and causes 'invalid JSON arguments' errors.

lunacode.temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0 is best for deterministic agentic coding.

enumDescriptions: Use the model's own default reasoning behavior.
Low thinking effort — faster and cheaper.
High thinking effort — most deliberate, slower and pricier.
lunacode.reasoningEffort.description: Thinking effort for reasoning-capable models, via OpenRouter's unified reasoning control. Lowering it (or Off) can curb runaway repeat-thinking loops; raising it helps hard problems.

lunacode.enablePromptCaching.description: Insert cache_control breakpoints on stable prefixes (system prompt, tools, prior turns) to maximize provider prompt-cache hits.

enumDescriptions: Deny — only route to OpenRouter providers that do NOT store or train on your prompts (recommended for code).
Allow — permit all providers, including ones that may retain or train on data.
lunacode.dataCollection.description: Restrict OpenRouter routing to providers that do not use your data for training.

lunacode.zeroDataRetention.description: Stricter than dataCollection: only route to Zero-Data-Retention (ZDR) endpoints. May reduce the set of available providers and occasionally cause routing failures if no ZDR endpoint exists for the model.

enumDescriptions: Throughput — prefer the fastest-generating provider. Fewer 'Upstream idle timeout' stalls on big contexts, but may route to pricier endpoints.
Latency — prefer the provider with the lowest time-to-first-token.
lunacode.providerSort.description: How OpenRouter picks among providers serving the model. Throughput/latency reduce provider stalls (upstream idle timeouts) at potentially higher cost per token.

lunacode.quantizations.description: Restrict routing to providers serving the model at these quantization levels. Empty = no restriction. To avoid low-precision (fp4) endpoints, list the acceptable levels, e.g. ["fp8","fp16","bf16","fp32"] (or just ["fp8"] to require exactly fp8). Providers not serving one of the listed quantizations are skipped.

lunacode.maxContextTokens.description: Context-budget trigger: when the conversation exceeds this many tokens, a compaction event summarizes older turns down to the floor (see compactionTargetRatio). 0 (recommended) sizes the budget automatically from the active model's context window AND its price, so a fully-cached turn stays under autoBudgetCarryCostUsd. Set a positive number to pin the budget manually.

lunacode.autoBudgetCarryCostUsd.description: Auto-budget target for the cost (USD) of one fully-cached context pass. Every API call re-reads the whole context (~0.1× input price when cached), so this caps the per-call carry cost on expensive models: at $0.10, a $10/M model is budgeted ~100K tokens while cheap models get their full window. Only used when maxContextTokens is 0.

lunacode.compactionTargetRatio.description: When a compaction event fires, the context is reduced to this fraction of the budget. Lower = rarer compactions (better prompt-cache economics) but more history summarized away.

lunacode.microcompactRatio.description: Soft context cleanup: when estimated tokens exceed this fraction of the budget, Luna Code stubs superseded/stale tool results and truncates large older outputs (content-only — one prompt-cache miss). 0 disables. Hard compaction (summarize/drop) still fires at the full budget.

lunacode.summarizerModel.description: OpenRouter model id used to summarize older history into a checkpoint during compaction (a cheap model like deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash is ideal). Empty = use the active session model. If the summarizer call fails, Luna Code falls back to plain truncation.

lunacode.subagentModel.description: OpenRouter model id for research sub-agents (explore tool — including parallel multi-question explores). A fast, cheap model works well here. Empty = use the currently selected session model.

lunacode.plannerModel.description: Cheap model for research/planning iterations of the main agent loop. When set, read-only phases route here; implement phases stay on the primary model. Empty = always use the session model. Often the same as subagentModel. Note: provider prompt caches are per-model, so each swap between planner and primary re-reads the conversation uncached on the other model — on long sessions the cheaper rate may not beat the lost cache hits.

lunacode.implementerModel.description: Model for the implement tool's write-capable sub-agent. Empty = use the session model (or subagentModel if you prefer a cheaper implementer).

lunacode.subagentMaxContextTokens.description: Token budget for explore/implement sub-agent contexts. When exceeded, the sub-agent is forced to answer from what it has. Lower = cheaper digests; higher = deeper research.

lunacode.progressiveTools.description: Start each turn with read/meta tool schemas only; unlock edit/exec tools after the first implement signal. Reduces fixed prompt tax on research-heavy turns. Tool-set changes only at iteration boundaries (cache-safe).

lunacode.adaptiveReasoning.description: Automatically lower reasoning effort on pure research follow-up iterations (saves cost on reasoning-capable models). Raises back when implementing.

lunacode.sessionBudgetUsd.description: Session spending limit in USD. When a session's total cost crosses this, Luna Code pauses and asks before continuing (even in Auto mode). 0 disables the guardrail.

lunacode.maxTurns.description: Maximum number of tool-loop steps the agent runs in a single turn before stopping. Higher values let the agent finish long tasks and plans in Auto mode without stalling. 0 = unlimited.

lunacode.loopGuardLimit.description: Soft-block a mutating tool call if the same file/command (or an identical call) is re-issued more than this many times in one turn — catches runaway second-guessing loops. The model gets a reason and can adapt; the turn only hard-stops after consecutive fully-blocked rounds. Does not apply to read_file paging. 0 = disabled.

lunacode.includeActiveFile.description: Attach the active editor's file path (and selection, if any) to each message you send, so the agent knows what you're looking at.

lunacode.formatAfterEdit.description: Run the workspace's document formatter on files right after the agent edits them, so its changes match project style.

lunacode.revealEditedFiles.description: As the agent edits files, reveal each one in a preview tab (without stealing focus from the chat) so you can watch changes land. Off by default — enable to follow along in the editor.

lunacode.worktreeMode.description: Sandbox mode: the agent works in a separate git worktree instead of your working tree. Review with the ± chip, then use 'Luna Code: Merge Sandbox Changes' to apply them to your real tree, or 'Discard Sandbox'. Note: dependencies (node_modules etc.) are not installed in the worktree.

lunacode.customCommands.markdownDescription: Custom slash commands: `{ "deploy": "Run the deploy checklist: ..." }` makes `/deploy` expand to that prompt (any text after the command is appended). Built-ins: `/commit`, `/review`, `/tests`.

lunacode.fallbackModels.description: Models to fall back to (in order) when the primary model errors or is rate-limited, via OpenRouter's models routing. A status line notes when a fallback served the response.

lunacode.favoriteModels.description: Your preferred model IDs (e.g. "anthropic/claude-sonnet-5"). When set, these are shown first in the model quick-pick (with an option to browse the full catalog). Empty = list all models.

lunacode.prewarmCache.description: Pre-warm the provider prompt cache when a session opens by sending a tiny request, so your first real message starts from a warm (cheap, fast) cache. Costs one small cache write you would have paid on the first message anyway.

lunacode.mcpServers.markdownDescription: MCP servers to connect via stdio. Each key is a server name mapping to `{ "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"], "env": { "GITHUB_TOKEN": "…" } }`. Their tools are exposed to the agent as `mcp__<server>__<tool>` and require approval in Standard mode.

lunacode.autoApproveCommands.description: Command prefixes auto-approved even in Standard mode.

lunacode.alwaysDenyCommands.description: Command substrings that are always blocked regardless of mode.

scripts.test: node dist/test-context.js && node dist/test-usage.js && node dist/test-checkpoints.js && node dist/test-loop-guard.js
